channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Roki Sasaki pulled from Dodger Stadium debut in 2nd inning after more struggles with his control
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Roki Sasaki's career with the Los Angeles Dodgers is not off to a sensational start. For a young pitcher who's been a dominant force at every other step in his meteoric baseball career, that's both a surprise and a challenge.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Duke's great D sends Blue Devils to their 18th Final Four with an 85-65 win over Alabama
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — Duke freshman Cooper Flagg swooped across the paint and swatted an Alabama player’s shot completely off the court. The ball landed in the stands. Flagg and the Blue Devils -- they’re headed to the Final Four.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Red Sox DH Rafael Devers is 1st big leaguer with 10 strikeouts in 3 games to open a season
item.description: Boston Red Sox designated hitter Rafael Devers has become the first major league player to strike out 10 times in the first three games of a season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: MJ Iraldi rallies Nova Southeastern past Cal State Dominguez Hills 74-73 in Division II title game
item.description: EVANSVILLE, Ind. (AP) — MJ Iraldi made two free throws with 17.8 seconds left and finished with 27 points to rally Nova Southeastern to a 74-73 victory over Cal State Dominguez Hills in the championship game of the Division II Tournament on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yankees record historic inning with home runs on three consecutive pitches
item.description: The New York Yankees had a historic first inning against the Milwaukee Brewers on Saturday, as they hit four home runs for the first time in franchise history.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jazz get blown out by Denver, 129-93
item.description: DENVER (ABC4 Sports) - One more loss by the Utah Jazz will assure the team of its worst record in franchise history, dating all the way back to 1974 in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Gianna Kneepkens enters transfer portal
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Gianna Kneepkens, the leading scorer on the Utah women's basketball team has announced she is entering the transfer portal.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Miguel Amaya has 2 doubles and 5 RBIs to lead the Cubs past the Diamondbacks 10-6
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Miguel Amaya hit two doubles and had five RBIs, Ian Happ added a homer, double and three RBIs and the Chicago Cubs beat the Arizona Diamondbacks 10-6 on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Sox fans shower Derrick Rose with MVP chants during first pitch
item.description: CHICAGO - Coming back to Chicago never gets old for Derrick Rose. The former Bulls MVP returned to the South Side Thursday to throw out the first pitch at the White Sox home opener, where he was showered with MVP chants from the crowd. "It's surreal. It's always surreal.
item.dc:creator: Rick Tarsitano, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox hammer Angels in lopsided Opening Day win
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — The weather on White Sox Opening Day mirrored their franchise outlook heading into 2025. Murky, gray and dreary with little to be excited about besides the fact baseball season is all the way back. Good thing the White Sox played well in spite of the melancholy conditions.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: Royal Riot Podcast Exclusive: Ariath Piol talks goals, red cards, and his journey to RSL
item.description: Royal Riot Podcast hosts Hayden and Mattey sit down with Ariath Piol in an exclusive interview where the player opens up about his journey to RSL, the emotions, lessons, and support he has received from the club and more.
item.dc:creator: Royal Riot Podcast, ABC 4

item.title: Loyola Chicago men's basketball team beats Kent State, advances to NIT semifinals
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Loyola Chicago men's basketball team didn't make the 68-team NCAA Tournament field this season, but the Ramblers are still ramblin' on to a different Final Four.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Opening Day 2025: Cubs have a winning Opening Day record, featuring some memorable moments
item.description: CHICAGO — Opening Day is finally upon us. It's a day that comes with a sense of hope among baseball fans in early spring, a season that represents new beginnings often exemplified by the warmer temperatures and the blooming of trees and flowers.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WGN 9

item.title: White Sox Opening Day: A lot of 'new' on the South Side in 2025. What to know if you're headed to Rate Field
item.description: CHICAGO — After a 2024 season that everyone would rather forget, there's a lot of "new" on the South Side as the White Sox get set to embark on their 2025 campaign Thursday. There's a new manager, Will Venable. There's a new stadium name, Rate Field. There's a new food and beverage menu.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: BYU expects shootout against Alabama in Sweet 16
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (ABC4 Sports) - The last time BYU was in the Sweet 16 was 2011 with Jimmer Fredette. For the local BYU players who grew up in Utah, they remember it well.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Rays prepare for Tampa home opener
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — The Tampa Bay Rays have their home opener for their season at Steinbrenner Field on Friday. The game is already a sell out. Seating capacity at this years games will be greatly reduced, due to major hurricane damage at Tropicana Field.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Patterson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rail service to and from Petco Park expands for Padres season
item.description: The North County Transit District is expanding COASTER service to provide fans with a safe and convenient way to get to and from Petco Park on Padres game days.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Bryan Hodgson named University of South Florida head men's basketball coach
item.description: Bryan Hodgson, one of the nation’s top recruiters and a rising head coach, has been named the University of South Florida head men’s basketball coach, Vice President for Athletics Michael Kelly announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Merissa Lynn, USF Athletics, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WWE legend Kane says Dems have 'real issue' with masculinity
item.description: WWE Hall of Famer Kane — also known as Glenn Jacobs, mayor of Knox County, Tennessee — joined "Morning in America.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: San Diego FC dealt first loss in inaugural season
item.description: San Diego FC lost its first game of the season thus far on Sunday when the club fell 2-1 to Austin FC at Q2 Stadium in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama rolls by Saint Mary's 80-66 to reach the NCAA Tournament's Sweet 16 for a 3rd straight year
item.description: CLEVELAND (AP) — Alabama turned the page on the program's first Final Four appearance the moment the players received their commemorative rings in October. Coach Nate Oats knew his team needed to get a little deeper and more athletic, and find more balance to go even further this season.
item.dc:creator: WILL GRAVES, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Tiger Woods confirms his relationship with Vanessa Trump in a social media post
item.description: Tiger Woods has confirmed his relationship with the former daughter-in-law of President Donald Trump, publishing two images to social media in a post that said, “Love is in the air.” Woods and Vanessa Trump, who was married to Donald Trump Jr.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FERGUSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New York girls basketball coach fired for hair-pulling incident with player apologizes
item.description: In a statement shared with local media, Zullo, 81, said he regretted his behavior and offered apologies to the player, her family, the team, the district, and the community.
item.dc:creator: Griffin Haas, NewsNation
